other_observers: Bryan Patrick; Found on 10/28/2008 by Cole Wild and Andrew Spenser, and later observed by Brad Steger and Mark Peterson.
specific_location: Near the dam at Antero Reservoir
description: This is a large loon with a double bump on its head, the proximal bump sticking up significantly. The diagnostic bill was pale, dull ivory-colored without a dark culmen. It angled noticeably upwards at the midway point on the lower edge of the lower mandible. The posture was distinct, with the entire head pointed at an upward angle while it swam. When well-lit, the face was light tan with a darker roundish auricualr patch. When in the shadows the darker auricular patch was difficult to see, and the overall coloration of the head was more brown. The eye was dark, and appeared smallish on the large face. The back was checkered. It appeared to be a large loon.Behaviors: Swam slowly near the dam, only diving when it had to pass near me by a point of land. Was not actively hunting.Call: none heardPlumage: juvenile
